posts.text:
Why is San Francisco spending taxpayer money on all-black drag shows and massage therapy for black criminals? Why is Gavin Newsom building a $114 million "butterfly bridge" and why is it so far over budget?

MI senior fellow @christopherrufo discusses this & more on @FoxBusiness

Video

Read the full brief here: 
manhattan.institute/article/…

New York City spends roughly $8 billion annually on nonprofit contracts—more than the entire NYPD budget—but sees limited accountability. A new MI issue brief by @joshjappel calls for a performance-based model tied to results.
Watch the entire episode and stay tuned for a new one dropping next week: piped.video/watch?v=dMWVc1zn…

ICYMI: MI's @tal_fortgang on today’s “civil terrorists” on the @CityJournal Podcast:

“It’s a broad way of describing a common thread: these are people who cast Americans and the U.S. government as villains in whatever they do.”

Video

"We have an affordability problem in New York City—that's undeniable." - @SantiVidalC.

Video

What forces are driving the ideological shift within America’s elite universities?

Join us April 13 for a conversation with @EliseStefanik on her new book Poisoned Ivies—and the stakes for academic freedom, intellectual diversity, and our civic culture.

MI's @PolicyEngineer on New York's current fiscal situation:

"More than half of the state budget goes to just two areas—Medicaid and school aid. Those are both areas where the state, historically, has done a terrible job making sure that it gets a good return on its investments."

Video

We’re excited to announce our new Immigration Calculator by @DanielDiMartino.

The tool allows users to build a custom immigration reform agenda using 48 policy options or explore seven pre-designed proposals, with projections for GDP, population, and the national debt.
Watch the entire episode and subscribe: piped.video/watch?v=vYMFcjC-…

"Our view was that cities are crucibles of innovation, economic growth, platforms for opportunities so people can rise in life." - @BrianAcity on the @CityJournal Podcast.

Video

"It's just devastating that so much ugliness, violence, and sadness can be directed at what is a house of worship but also a preschool housing a bunch of little American Jewish boys and girls." - @Jesse_Leg on the Michigan synagogue attack.

Video

MI's @Rafa_Mangual on @BigBenShowFox on the attack outside Gracie Mansion:
 
“When you have so many of these flags attached to a single event, it cannot be policed just by precinct officers and community affairs. What you really need is some kind of counterterrorism element.”

Video
